# Hypotheses by creative lever

Use this file when deciding which creative variable to isolate in a test.

| ID | Area / form | Hypothesis | Primary tactic | Best metric | First test |
|---:|---|---|---|---|---|
| H316 | First frame | The best first frame shows either result, problem, proof or identity; generic context loses. | result/problem/proof frame | chose-to-view | four first-frame variants |
| H317 | First frame | First frames with labelled contrast outperform single-state frames for before/after content. | labelled contrast | 1-sec hold | single result vs before/after split |
| H318 | First frame | A first frame should be designed after choosing the primary action: save, send, comment, follow or search. | action-led first frame | target action rate | same content with action-specific first frame |
| H319 | Hook | Hooks should name a concrete gap, not vague suspense. | specific information gap | completion | specific hook vs vague hook |
| H320 | Hook | Contrarian hooks require proof within 5–10 seconds to preserve trust. | contrarian + proof | comments + follows | contrarian no proof vs proof early |
| H321 | Hook | Result-first hooks work best when the result is visually shown, not just claimed. | show the result | 3-sec hold | spoken result vs visible result |
| H322 | Captions | Edited captions beat auto captions when speech density is high. | corrected captions | avg % viewed | auto vs edited |
| H323 | Captions | Captions should summarise speech, not transcribe every filler word. | clean captions | completion | verbatim vs cleaned captions |
| H324 | Captions | Caption placement should follow visual safe zones rather than fixed lower-centre placement. | dynamic safe-zone placement | completion | fixed vs dynamic caption placement |
| H325 | Text overlays | Overlay text works when it states the frame's job: problem, step, proof, result or CTA. | functional text | completion | decorative vs functional text |
| H326 | Text overlays | Huge central overlay text performs worse over dynamic footage than over static or low-motion footage. | text-motion fit | completion | central text on dynamic vs static footage |
| H327 | Text overlays | Text density should decrease as visual detail increases. | density matching | avg watch | same text over simple vs complex visuals |
| H328 | Face | Unknown creators benefit from early face presence if followed by proof quickly. | face → proof | 3-sec hold + saves | face late vs face early |
| H329 | Face | Facecam improves software tutorials when it adds trust but does not cover the UI target. | small facecam safe-zone | saves | no facecam vs obstructive vs safe facecam |
| H330 | Face | Emotional face reactions improve shares when the emotional reaction is the content's social value. | reaction framing | sends/shares | no reaction vs reaction |
| H331 | Audio | Voice clarity matters more than trend audio for educational videos. | voice-first mix | completion | trend loud vs clean voice |
| H332 | Audio | Sound effects should punctuate semantic beats, not every cut. | meaningful SFX | avg watch | random SFX vs beat SFX |
| H333 | Audio | Silence/low audio can increase seriousness for proof-led content if captions are strong. | quiet proof tone | saves, completion | music bed vs quiet proof |
| H334 | Pacing | Cuts should happen when information changes, not when the editor gets bored. | semantic cuts | avg watch | decorative vs information cuts |
| H335 | Pacing | Educational videos need slower pacing during proof inspection and faster pacing during setup. | variable pacing | completion | constant fast vs variable pace |
| H336 | Pacing | The first 10 seconds should contain at least two meaning changes for cold-audience educational content. | early beat density | 3-sec/10-sec retention | one beat vs two/three beats |
| H337 | Story | Problem-test-result beats pure advice when the advice is not obvious. | experiment story | completion, saves | advice vs test story |
| H338 | Story | Before-after-why is the strongest structure when transformation is visible. | transformation story | saves, shares | tips vs before-after-why |
| H339 | Story | Myth-evidence-correction increases comments but needs a replacement framework to earn saves. | myth + replacement | comments + saves | myth-only vs myth + framework |
| H340 | Proof | Proof shown early improves trust even if it slightly lowers casual reach. | proof-first | saves/follows | claim-first vs proof-first |
| H341 | Proof | Real examples outperform abstract diagrams for beginner audiences. | example-first | completion | diagram vs real example |
| H342 | Proof | Framework diagrams outperform examples for expert audiences if the example is already obvious. | framework abstraction | saves | example-only vs framework |
| H343 | CTA | CTA should match the content's natural action: save for utility, send for social relevance, comment for choice, follow for series. | action-fit CTA | target action | save/send/comment/follow variants |
| H344 | CTA | Early soft CTAs can guide attention without feeling like engagement bait. | attention CTA | avg watch | no mid prompt vs 'watch this drop' |
| H345 | CTA | A CTA loses power when it asks for two actions at once. | single action CTA | action rate | save+follow vs save only |
| H346 | Length | Length should expand only when each extra segment adds proof, tension or payoff. | value-density audit | avg % viewed | short vs extended with proof vs padded |
| H347 | Length | Very short videos win completion but may lose saves if they omit enough reusable detail. | short utility balance | completion + saves | 8s vs 25s vs 45s |
| H348 | Length | Longer short-form videos need visible progress markers to prevent perceived drift. | progress labels | completion | 90s no labels vs labels |
| H349 | Cover / title | Covers matter most after feed exposure, when viewers visit the profile or search surface. | profile/search cover | profile watch rate | aesthetic vs explanatory cover |
| H350 | Cover / title | Titles should be clearer than hooks for search surfaces and more tensioned for feed surfaces. | surface-specific title | search + feed performance | search title vs feed title |
| H351 | Cover / title | Positive or useful wording in titles may preserve trust better than exaggerated negative wording. | trustful packaging | completion/follows | negative vs useful title |
| H352 | Comment system | Comment prompts should collect usable research variables, not empty engagement. | research comment prompt | comment quality | generic vs useful prompt |
| H353 | Comment system | Top comments can become proof or objections for the next video. | comment-to-content loop | return viewers | new idea from comments vs unrelated idea |
| H354 | Comment system | Pinned comments can steer interpretation and reduce repeated confusion. | pinned clarification | sentiment, saves | no pin vs clarifying pin |
| H355 | Originality / AI workflow | AI-generated content needs a visible original judgment layer to avoid template fatigue. | human judgment layer | follows, sentiment | generic AI vs AI + real examples |
| H356 | Originality / AI workflow | Original tests beat recycled claims for trust-building niches. | experimental proof | saves/follows | common advice vs original test |
| H357 | Originality / AI workflow | Creator-specific constraints and examples make AI-assisted scripts feel less generic. | specificity injection | completion, comments | generic script vs specific local example |
